To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix. The characteristic polynomial is given by $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

### Step 1: Simplify the matrix by factoring out a common denominator
Notice that all entries have a denominator that is a factor of 16. Let's rewrite the matrix by factoring out $\frac{1}{16}$.
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Let $B$ be the integer matrix inside the parentheses:
$$ B = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Then $A = \frac{1}{16} B$.
The characteristic polynomial of $A$ is:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det\left(\frac{1}{16} B - \lambda I\right) $$
We can factor out $\frac{1}{16}$ from each row (or column) of the matrix inside the determinant. Since it is a $3 \times 3$ matrix, factoring out $\frac{1}{16}$ from each of the 3 rows gives a factor of $\left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^3 =
